prompts_findings.prompt_lead: Review this session and sharpen the generator's tactic beyond what the loop reached.

risks_fired.evidence: "Then rewrite ./generation-tactic.md. Integrate, don't append." — no explicit overwrite-the-existing-file instruction; Claude will likely ask 'overwrite or create new?' before writing.
risks_fired.fix_hint: Add: 'Overwrite ./generation-tactic.md in place — do not create a new file.'

risks_fired.evidence: "Then rewrite ./generation-tactic.md. Integrate, don't append." — integration mode is named but overwrite/create-new boundary is implicit; a student who gets a second file (generation-tactic-v2.md) loses the artifact chain.
risks_fired.fix_hint: Pair the integrate instruction with an explicit overwrite command naming the destination path.

risks_fired.evidence: "which tactic change helped, which one did not help and should be removed or rewritten" — Claude reviews tactic decisions it authored; RLHF charity toward its own prior work inflates the 'helped' count and shrinks the 'should be removed' list.
risks_fired.fix_hint: Add: 'Be harsher than feels comfortable — if a rule is vague, say so by name; if it fired on the wrong thing, name what it fired on.'

risks_fired.evidence: "tell me in 3–5 lines: what you added, what you sharpened, what you removed, and why" — self-summary of own tactic rewrite will inflate sharpening count and undercount removals; the student has no artifact-level check prompt to counter this.
risks_fired.fix_hint: After the prompt, add a copyable push-back: 'Which rule did you claim to remove but actually kept under a different name? Be specific.'

risks_fired.evidence: "remove a rule that fired on the wrong thing" — prompt demands removal, but RLHF drift will prefer renaming or narrowing the rule rather than deleting it; no line is pinned as non-negotiable.
risks_fired.fix_hint: Pin the expected outcome: 'A removal means the rule is gone from the file, not renamed or scoped — if you scoped it instead, say so and why.'

risks_fired.evidence: Multi-part prompt with a read-then-rewrite structure; Claude will open with a narrated summary of what it is about to do before reading the first file.
risks_fired.fix_hint: Prime the student in the surrounding prose: 'Claude will narrate the review before acting; skim past the opening and look for the rewritten tactic file.'
